findings.content: - Never: `pathian`, `ram`, `fapa`, `bawipa`, `siangpahrang`, `cu/cun`
- Never: `pathian`, `ram`, `fapa`, `bawipa`, `siangpahrang`, `cu/cun`
- Never: `pathian`, `ram`, `fapa`, `bawipa`, `siangpahrang`, `cu/cun`
- Never: `pathian`, `ram`, `fapa`, `bawipa`, `siangpahrang`, `cu/cun`
- Never: `pathian`, `ram`, `fapa`, `bawipa`, `siangpahrang`, `cu/cun`
- Never: `pathian`, `ram`, `fapa`, `bawipa`, `siangpahrang`, `cu/cun`
- Never: `pathian`, `ram`, `fapa`, `bawipa`, `siangpahrang`, `cu/cun`
- `amaute pai uh hi` = they went (NOT `amaute i pai hi`)
| `I pai uh hi` | `I pai hi` (never uh with i) |
- `[TDB77]` *Now these things were our examples, to the intent we should not lus
⚠️ NEVER: I pai uh hi. — WRONG (uh + i = forbidden)
| **siangpahrang** | **kumpipa** | King / Ruler |
- **Rule:** NEVER combine `uh` with `i`. (Khanggui lines 474–481)
Hong daisak nuam, mawh ong zongte in, bangbang ihi zongin paulap tuamtuam nei nu
Hong gensia dingte in bangbang i sem i bawl zong, hong gensia veve ding uh hi. M
- Plural: never combine `uh` with `i` (we) — `I pai hi` ✅, `I pai uh hi` ❌
vive suaksak uh ahih manin, a khiatna tuampi khat ahihlam i phawkkhak ding deihn
Note: “i” zatna ah, “uh” cih tawh zatkhop kullo hi. “ihi hi”, “i kizasak hi” hil
- Never: `pathian`, `ram`, `fapa`, `bawipa`, `siangpahrang`, `cu/cun`

discussion_group.consensus: All wiki examples and documentation require updates for ZVS 2018 compliance and completeness
